design_philosophy.core_loop: Shoot enemies, navigate platforming sequences, utilize grappling arm to swing between platforms and reach power-ups, progress through vehicle-based and on-foot combat sections
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing offensive firepower with precise platforming navigation; managing ammunition and power-ups while facing enemy waves
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Elite commando in advanced assault suit fighting through a sci-fi dystopia to stop an alien threat; piloting experimental vehicles across varied devastated worlds
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately shifts away from the original Turrican's exploration-heavy philosophy toward tighter, more Contra-like linear action. The addition of the grappling arm mechanic (inspired by Bionic Commando) defines a unique movement identity within run-and-gun genre. Mode 7 effects and vehicle segments showcase technical ambition for SNES platform.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden level select cheat code available during gameplay, suggesting developers acknowledged difficulty and offered legitimate skip mechanism for players

progression.upgrade_system: Power-up collection system; grappling arm grants access to previously unreachable areas and power-ups
progression.unlock_mechanism: Level progression through sequential world completion; vehicles unlock for specific stage segments
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select enables non-linear approach to content
Varied vehicle segments (dune buggy, space motorcycle, underwater motojet) provide gameplay variety across levels
Different battlefield settings encourage exploration of stage design

world_building_techniques: Opening text crawl establishing sci-fi premise: star system under siege by The Machine
Narrative framing: sole survivor of assault squad crashes on devastated dune world
Environmental design conveys apocalyptic setting through 'devastated realm' architecture and landscape destruction
Vehicle segments suggest different planetary environments and tactical scenarios

faction_indicators: The Machine (antagonist faction, returning villain from previous conflict)
Generic alien/machine enemy forces occupying star system
danger_signals: Black hole as environmental hazard threatening player ship
Phasers launched from deep space destroying civilizations
The Machine's stronghold occupying multiple planets
environmental_storytelling: Dune world landscape showing signs of bombardment and destruction
Varied battlefield settings reflecting conquered/corrupted planets
Vehicle availability suggests different tactical zones (desert, space, underwater)
Wreckage of player's assault fleet establishing military conflict scale

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Super Turrican 2
